vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on shadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/shadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/shadow

triage_decision.reason: Changing file permissions on /etc/shadow is a standard STIG remediation that does not require service restarts or system reboots. Root can always read the file, so there is no risk of lockout. The fix is a simple chmod command, fitting the policy for safe remediation.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Incorrect permissions could prevent authentication if set improperly; Potential impact on services that read /etc/shadow during runtime
